differentials.name: cystic lesion with tumor nodule
differentials.raw: cystic lesion with tumor nodule ("mural nodule") in cyst wall (50%); (midline astrocytomas cystic in 50%, hemispheric astrocytomas cystic in 80%)

differentials.name: solid mass with cystic
differentials.raw: solid mass with cystic (= necrotic) center (40-45%)

differentials.name: hydrocephalus, headache, vomiting, neck pain, 6th nerve palsy
differentials.raw: hydrocephalus, headache, vomiting, neck pain, 6th nerve palsy

differentials.name: cerebellar signs: truncal ataxia, dysdiadochokinesia appendicular dysmetria, gait disturbance Location:   originating in midline with extension into cerebellar hemisphere
differentials.raw: cerebellar signs: truncal ataxia, dysdiadochokinesia appendicular dysmetria, gait disturbance Location:   originating in midline with extension into cerebellar hemisphere (29-53%), vermis (16-71%) > tonsils > brainstem (34%)

differentials.name: 40% 25-year survival rate for solid cerebellar astrocytoma
differentials.raw: 40% 25-year survival rate for solid cerebellar astrocytoma

differentials.name: 90% 25-year survival rate for cystic juvenile pilocytic astrocytoma
differentials.raw: 90% 25-year survival rate for cystic juvenile pilocytic astrocytoma

differentials.name: Cyst with intensely enhancing mural nodule
differentials.raw: Cyst with intensely enhancing mural nodule (67%) (a)  nonenhancing cyst wall (21%) (b)  enhancing cyst wall (46%)

differentials.name: Solid mass
differentials.raw: Solid mass (33%) (a)  central nonenhancing necrotic zone (16%) (b)  minimal / no cystic component (17%)

differentials.name: diencephalic syndrome
differentials.raw: diencephalic syndrome (= emaciation despite normal / slightly decreased caloric intake, alert appearance, hyperkinesis, irritability, normal / accelerated growth)

context: Incidence:   70-75% of all primary intracranial tumors; most common brain tumor in children (40-50% of all primary pediatric intracranial neoplasms) Distribution:   proportional to amount of white matter Location: cerebral hemisphere (lobar), thalamus, pons, midbrain, may spread across corpus callosum; no particular lobar distribution (a)  in adults: central white matter of cerebrum (15-30% of all gliomas) (b)  in children: cerebellum (40%), brainstem (20%), supratentorial (30%) Well-differentiated = Low-grade Astrocytoma Incidence:  9% of all primary intracranial tumors; 10-15% of gliomas Age
